mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a univariate regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase MoM
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive slope is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 2.0% confirms inflation is subdued, prompting immediate market repricing of lower future rate hikes and generating positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL falls below 2.0 over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical fluctuation, which erodes long-term earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to separate positive from negative 60-day returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, providing a persistent positive drift to S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term noise.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth across all available historical windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid consumer demand normalization, which immediately boosts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration during recovery periods fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10.
